default: Weary from the road? I've a bed upstairs whenever you need it.
Rest as long as you like — the fire stays warm.
A quiet room and a sound sleep, that's what I offer.
Sleep here and you'll wake to a brighter day.
Good day, traveler!
The harvest was good this year.
Have you seen the guards today?
I heard strange noises from the woods last night...
Welcome to our village!

lines: Always a pleasure to see you, friend!
Our hero returns! What can we do for you?
You are welcome at my table any time.
Stay away from me!
I know what you did. Everyone knows.
We don't want your kind here.
Hard times, traveler. The larders are thin.
If things don't turn around soon, folk will start leaving.
We could use any help we can get.
Never seen the village do so well!
Good times — the stalls are full and so are the bellies.
You're up late, traveler.
Quiet night, isn't it?
Can't chat long — work to do.
These fields won't tend themselves.

default: Move along, citizen.
Stay out of trouble.
The roads aren't safe at night.
I'm keeping watch. No need to worry.
Report anything suspicious to me.

lines: You've a nerve showing your face to the watch.
Murderer. The next blade you see will be mine.
Step wrong and I'll cut you down where you stand.
The watch is glad to have you, friend.
You've earned our respect. Walk easy.
Any trouble, and the guard stands with you.

default: Looking to buy something?
I just got a new shipment in!
Best prices in the village, I guarantee it.
Come back anytime!
Trade keeps the village alive.

lines: Shelves are half empty — deliveries keep getting raided.
Business is terrible. Bring me goods and I'll pay fairly.
For you? Special prices, of course!
My best customer! Come in, come in.
Your coin is good, but I'm watching you.
Buy something or get out.

default: Welcome to our humble village.
I try my best to keep the peace around here.
Please, cause no trouble during your stay.
The recent monster attacks have been concerning.
If you can help defend us, you'll be well rewarded.

lines: You've done it? Step inside — let's settle what you're owed.
Back already, and with good news I hope. Come, come.
Word reached me before you did. The council is grateful.
Any progress on the matter we discussed?
The task still stands, and folk are counting on you.
No news yet? The errand won't run itself, traveler.

blacksmith: Strike while the iron is hot!
Armor or weapons, you name it, I can craft it.
Keep your gear in good condition out there.
_default: They don't seem interested in talking.
_gossip: Have you seen {subject} about? Keeping to themselves lately.
{subject} swears the harvest will be better next year.
They say {subject} fancies someone across the square.
Word is {subject} had words with the mayor again.
Cold nights lately. My old bones feel every one.
The work's hard, but the roof holds, eh?
Heard the price of bread again? Robbery, I call it.
Quiet today. Quiet's good. Quiet means no wolves.
_gossip_friend: Good soul, {subject}. Always lends a hand at harvest.
I'd trust {subject} with my last coin, and gladly.
{subject} sat with me through the fever last winter. Never forget that.
If you need help, ask {subject} — kindest in the village.
{subject} and I go back years. Salt of the earth, that one.
_gossip_rival: Don't get me started on {subject}. Still owes me for the spring market.
{subject}? Wouldn't share water in a drought, that one.
Mark my words, {subject} shorted me on the last trade.
I've no quarrel with anyone — save {subject}, and they know why.
Keep your purse shut around {subject}, is all I'll say.

default: Tracks everywhere this season. Wolves, mostly.
A clean shot and a quiet step — that's all the trade needs.
The wilds give to those who respect them.

lines: Night hunts are for owls, not people.

farmer: The fields don't wait for anyone.
Rain when we need sun, sun when we need rain. Always the way.
Buy local, stranger. Keeps us all fed.

default: Sage for the lungs, yarrow for the blood.
Fresh salves, just stirred. Better than any town potion.
If it grows in the swamp, I can brew with it.

lines: Half my customers can't pay — I trade for eggs now.

ore_digger: Bog iron's good iron, whatever the city smiths say.
Knee-deep in the mire all day — someone has to be.
The smithy in Eastmoor buys every lump we haul.
traveling_merchant: Roads are long, purses are short. Let's fix one of those.
Everything you see is for sale. Almost everything.
I hear all sorts of things on the road, friend.
